title: road-to-master research-note evidence ledger
description: Per-note provenance ledger. Every non-'unknown'/'unavailable' Academic Context table cell, every referenced figure, and every non-trivial note claim MUST have a matching entry. Validated offline (shape/coverage only) by tools/research/check_note.py — snippet TRUTH (re-fetch) is a Task 02 ProvenanceCritic concern, out of scope here.

note_path.description: Repo-relative path to the note, e.g. domains/<domain>/<Topic>/README.md

domain.description: One of the fixed domains (enum is derived at runtime from live domains/ dirs): natural_language_processing, computer_vision, recommender_system, timeseries, reinforcement_learning, mlops, utils, vision_language

topic.description: Sanitized <Topic> folder name; MUST match ^[A-Za-z0-9._-]+$ (no '..', no path separators, no shell metachars).

no_untrusted_code_executed.description: Attestation that no untrusted code was executed while producing the note. The gate requires literal true.

generated_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the ledger was generated, or null when unknown.

entries.description: Claim/provenance entries. May be empty ONLY for a note that makes no claims; check_note.py fails ledger:coverage when the body contains substantive claim material without the exact required note-section:<body-kind-N> target and a snippet/text matching that body unit.

claim_id.description: Stable id, unique within the ledger.

claim_text.description: The claim / cell value being backed.

kind.description: 4-way epistemic taxonomy (a 2-way split is a FAIL). paper-claim = what the paper asserts, not yet judged; evidence-supported = backed by verifiable evidence (paper data/figure, a cited source, or a read code file); reasonable-inference = the note author's reasoned inference, explicitly labeled; unproven-or-doubtful = flagged as not established / questionable.

target.description: What this entry backs. Conventions the gate understands: 'table-cell:<normalized-field>' (Academic Context cell; field lowercased, spaces->hyphens), 'figure:imgs/<file>' (a referenced figure), or 'note-section:<body-kind-N>' for deterministic first-principles body claim units (body-prose-1, body-equation-1, body-table-1, body-code-1, ...). A code claim uses a target containing 'code'.

source_url.description: Provenance. Substantive Academic Context cells and non-code note-section entries with kind paper-claim/evidence-supported require a non-empty source_url. For an evidence-supported CODE claim (target contains 'code') this MUST be a filesystem path (matching ^(\.?/|[^:]+/), never a URL ^[a-z]+://) that resolves to a real file in the inspected clone (note dir or repo root); null / a pathless string / a nonexistent path all FAIL the code-source gate. May be null for reasonable-inference / unproven-or-doubtful entries.

retrieved_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when source_url was retrieved, or null.

quoted_snippet.description: Verbatim snippet substantiating the claim. Must contain no secrets.
